chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI C.P No. D-4927 of 2022 ______________________________________________________ DATE: ORDER WITH SIGNATURE(S) OF JUDGE(S). ____________________________________________________________ Direction For order as to maintainability of Petition. 31.08.2022 Mr. Abdul Wahab Baloch, Advocate for the Petitioner alongwith Mr. Jamil Ahmed Shah and Muhammad Imran Qureshi, Advocates. ------------------- YOUSUF ALI SAYEED, J. The captioned Petition under Article 199 of the Constitution has been preferred in the backdrop of the widespread flooding that has recently ensued in the province as a consequence of the unprecedentedly heavy rainfall across the country, with it being alleged that the concerned authorities at the Provincial level are failing to properly address the situation. On that premise, it has been prayed that the official respondents be directed to release funds amongst the persons affected across the province while produce details of all those persons as well as the “sketch/layout plan” for the precautionary steps that have been taken and the record of the funds provided and disbursed to date. However, a perusal of the Petition reflects that the pleadings are vague and ambiguous, and while entire matter is predicated on the alleged failure of the official machinery at the Provincial level, no specific instance of such failure has even been cited. Under such circumstances, no case for issuance of writ stands made out, and the Petition is accordingly dismissed in limine, along with the other pending miscellaneous applications. JUDGE CHIEF JUSTICE TariqAli/PA
